_meta.title: Tomsgarden game rules specification
_meta._notes: Single source of truth for all build agents. Mechanics are identical to Azul: Queen's Garden (by Michael Kiesling, Plan B Games). Numbers are taken from the official EN rulebook to preserve balance. Component NAMES used here are the original Queen's Garden terms for clarity; see rename-map.json for the Tomsgarden public-facing names. Game mechanics are not copyrightable; only specific names and art are. Do NOT ship trademarked names or art.

rounds._notes: Each round = Phase 1 (player actions until all pass), Phase 2 (scoring via rotary wheel), Phase 3 (prepare next round; skipped after round 4, replaced by final scoring).

tileSet._notes: 6 patterns are distributed evenly across the 6 colors. 108 colored tiles total = 6 patterns x 6 colors x 3 copies each. Each unique pattern+color combination is called a 'hexagon' and exists 3 times among the tiles (jokers are separate/wild).
tileSet._patternValueNote: Each pattern has a single value used BOTH as its placement cost AND as its per-hexagon point value during scoring. tree=1, bird=2, butterfly=3, flower=4, fifth=5, sixth=6.

jokers.supply: unlimited (substitute if physical supply runs out)
jokers._notes: Jokers are wild/grey tiles. They occupy tile-storage spaces. A joker may be used to pay any part of a placement cost EXCEPT it cannot be the hexagon actually placed in the garden. Surrounding a feature awards jokers.

startingSetup._notes: CONFIRMED from official EN rulebook p.2 'Game Setup'. Each player starts from scratch: an EMPTY garden except for the single fountain board placed in its middle.

perPlayer._scoreNote: CONFIRMED: scoring marker starts on the 15th space of the scoring track (rulebook setup step 3). Players begin with 15 points so they can afford the 6-point face-down expansion purchase early.

fountainBoardGeometry.shape: roughly circular hex cluster around a central fountain feature
fountainBoardGeometry._unconfirmed: 13 hex spaces is confirmed by multiple sources (Zatu Games how-to-play: 'fountain boards are larger than the garden expansions, 13 hexagons instead of 7'). The exact arrangement of the 13 hexes around the fountain art and whether additional features (e.g. a bench/statue) are printed on it must be verified from board photos. The fountain itself sits in/at the center and is a surroundable feature.
fountainBoardGeometry.suggestedAxialLayout: _unconfirmed: a 13-space layout consistent with photos is a hex ring structure: central fountain occupying the middle with 13 tile spaces arranged around/beside it. Engine may use any 13-space connected hex layout with the fountain feature adjacent to several of them; flag for art verification.

sharedSetup.wheelStartPosition: first quadrant (dents aligned to the two markers at top)
sharedSetup.firstPlayer: youngest player, marker placed in display area

gardenExpansions._notes: CONFIRMED count (rulebook setup step 5: 'Shuffle the 36 garden expansions'). Garden expansions are double-sided hex pieces. Face DOWN (back): blank spaces only. Face UP (front, revealed when emptied of tiles in the display): shows a pavilion in the center and ONE printed hexagon (pattern+color) on one of its spaces. They are drafted like tiles (storage limit 2) and placed into the garden to add space.
gardenExpansions._spacesNote: CONFIRMED (rulebook Action C): 'Garden expansions provide 5 or 7 additional spaces for tiles.' A face-down expansion bought from the supply for 6 points provides 7 FREE spaces (no printed hexagon, no pavilion visible).

countBySize._unconfirmed: The split of the 36 expansions between 5-space and 7-space pieces is not stated in the rulebook text and could not be confirmed from secondary sources. Verify by counting from component photos/BGG files. Engine note: face-down supply purchases are explicitly 7-space, so at least a substantial share are 7-space.

faceUpFeatures.otherFeatures: _unconfirmed: some expansions show additional features (statues, benches) in photos; exact distribution of statue/bench/fountain features across the 36 expansions is not documented in the rulebook text and must be taken from component scans.

geometry._unconfirmed: Exact hex outlines of the 5-space and 7-space pieces are art-defined. A 7-space piece consistent with photos is a centered hex 'flower' (1 center + 6 ring) with the pavilion occupying the center position graphic; a 5-space piece is an irregular pentomino-like hex cluster. Verify before finalizing client geometry.
geometry.placement: Attach on a free expansion area around the fountain board, any orientation, following the same adjacency rules as tiles (rulebook Action C, CONFIRMED).

gardenExpansions._stackNote: CONFIRMED (rulebook setup step 5). Shuffle all 36 expansions and build 4 face-down round stacks of this size. Remaining expansions form the supply with the shield/cost (-6) token on top. Supply size = 36 - 4*stackSize: 16 (2p), 8 (3p), 4 (4p).

placementCosts._notes: Cost to place a tile/expansion equals the VALUE of its pattern. The hexagon being placed COUNTS toward its own cost, so you discard (cost - 1) additional hexagons from storage. All discarded hexagons (including the placed one) must be either all same pattern/different colors OR all same color/different patterns, never two identical hexagons. Jokers may substitute for any of the additional needed hexagons.

storage._notes: Jokers count against the 12 tile spaces. You may not perform an acquire that would exceed available space.

fountainBoard.placedAt: center of player's garden at setup
fountainBoard._notes: CONFIRMED: the fountain board is the ONLY garden piece a player starts with (rulebook setup step 1). It has 13 hexagon tile spaces (Zatu: '13 hexagons instead of 7') and a fountain feature. The garden is otherwise EMPTY at game start. See startingSetup section.

gardenBoard._layoutNotes: The garden grows organically: players attach garden-expansion pieces (5 or 7 hex each) around the central fountain board. Tiles are placed onto hex spaces of the fountain board and expansions. Adjacency is hex-based (up to 6 neighbors per hex).

placementRules.adjacencyRule: A placed tile must either have NO adjacent hexagons, OR share the same pattern OR the same color with at least one adjacent hexagon.
placementRules.noIdenticalAdjacent: You may never create or extend a group such that two identical hexagons (same pattern AND same color) become adjacent.
placementRules._notes: Garden expansions placed into the garden follow the same adjacency rules as tiles, in the orientation of the player's choice.

features._notes: Garden features are decorative elements on the boards. Fully surrounding a feature (all its adjacent hex spaces occupied) immediately awards jokers from the supply, placed into storage; excess jokers (no storage space) are lost.
features._jokerSourceNote: Rulebook text only says 'you receive jokers from the supply according to the surrounded element'; the exact numbers are printed on the bottom-left of the player board (Zatu Games how-to-play: 'the player immediately receives as many jokers as depicted on the bottom left side of the player board'). The Tabletop Crier review states pavilions award the MOST at 3 jokers ('Pavilions were the best at three joker tokens'), inverting the earlier placeholder ordering. Fountain is the rarest (1 per player, printed on start board) so it logically awards fewest; pavilions appear on every flipped expansion but require surrounding 6 spaces of a 7-space piece.

types._unconfirmed: Verify against player-board art. Only confirmed datum: pavilion = 3 (max). Fountain/statue/bench exact values unverified; 1/2/2-style guesses pending photo confirmation.

types._sourceConfirmedPartial: 3 jokers for surrounding a pavilion is reported by The Tabletop Crier review ('Pavilions were the best at three joker tokens'). The +1 point per visible pavilion at round scoring is CONFIRMED by the rulebook.

jokerRules.asPayment: May replace any additional needed hexagon when paying a placement cost. Cannot be the hexagon placed in the garden.
jokerRules.fromSurroundingFeatures: Awarded immediately when a feature is fully surrounded; placed into storage; excess lost.
jokerRules._notes: Jokers are wild for COST purposes only. They are NOT placed onto the garden and do not score patterns/colors during scoring (a joker placed nowhere in garden). At final scoring each remaining joker in storage = +1 point.

roundScoring._notes: Phase 2. The rotary wheel indicates 3 scored patterns OR colors for the current round. For each hexagon in your garden matching an indicated pattern or color you score that hexagon's pattern VALUE in points. A single hexagon can score twice (once for matching pattern, once for matching color). Then +1 point per visible pavilion.
roundScoring.perHexagonPoints: equals the pattern value of the matching hexagon (tree=1 ... sixth=6)

rotaryWheel._notes: The wheel has 4 quadrants, one per round, each pointing at 3 of the 12 pattern/color categories. Points scale UP from round 1 to round 4 because later rounds tend to target higher-value patterns and you have more matching hexagons placed. The exact 3 categories per quadrant depend on which physical wheel side is used (two sides for variability).
rotaryWheel._constraintConfirmed: CONFIRMED (Zatu Games how-to-play): 'each pattern and colour are scored only once per game' and 'the points that could be gained increase progressively from round one to four.' Therefore the 4 quadrants x 3 categories = exactly the 12 categories (6 patterns + 6 colors), each appearing once, with expected value rising round to round (low-value patterns early, high-value patterns late).
rotaryWheel._unconfirmed: The precise 3-category-per-quadrant assignment is encoded in the physical wheel art and not listed in any rulebook text or located written source. Partial datum (BGG thread 3130788): on the side used by that poster, ROUND 1 scores dark green and blue tiles (two colors), implying a colors-heavy round 1. The assignment below honors all confirmed constraints (each category once; values ramp up; round 1 = pattern1 + 2 colors per the BGG hint pattern) but the specific color/pattern slots must be VERIFIED against wheel photos before competitive balancing.

passPenalty._notes: First player to pass takes the first-player marker and moves back 1 space on the scoring track. On passing, a player MAY discard any number of hexagons from storage and score their values as MINUS points (voluntary cleanup).

buyExpansionFromSupply._notes: Action C alternative: spend exactly 6 points on the scoring track to take a face-down expansion (7 free spaces). Score cannot go negative.

finalScoring._notes: After the 4th round's normal end-of-round scoring, perform final scoring.

emptyStorage.perRemainingTileOrExpansion: minus the pattern value (tree=-1, bird=-2, ... sixth=-6)

finalScoring.groupDefinition: A group is 2+ adjacent hexagons sharing the same pattern OR the same color (not both). One hexagon can belong to a pattern-group and a color-group simultaneously. A group may never contain two identical hexagons.
finalScoring.evaluationOrder: Evaluate each of the 6 colors top-to-bottom, then each of the 6 patterns, using the evaluation track.

groupScoring._notes: Each group of at least 3 hexagons matching the current color/pattern evaluation scores. A group is scored by summing the pattern VALUE of each hexagon in the group. There is no limit to how many groups score per evaluation, and the same tile can count in multiple groups (once per color-group, once per pattern-group).
groupScoring.scorePerGroup: sum of pattern values of all hexagons in the group

completeSetBonus._notes: 6 additional bonus points for each group of 6 hexagons (a complete set). This is on top of the group's summed-value score.
completeSetBonus._taskInterpretation: Task brief simplification: 'groups of >=3 score 3, complete set of six scores 6 bonus, tiles can count in multiple groups.' The rulebook's actual rule is richer (groups score the SUM of member pattern values, not a flat 3). Both the flat-3 brief value and the rulebook sum-of-values rule are documented; the engine should implement the rulebook SUM rule for fidelity. Flag: if the design intends the simplified flat-3, switch scorePerGroup to a constant 3.

turnFlow._notes: One action per turn, clockwise, until all players have passed.

acquire.rule: Declare a pattern OR a color, then take ALL tiles AND garden expansions in the display showing it, but skip duplicates (take only one of any identical hexagon). Selected pattern => all different colors; selected color => all different patterns.
acquire.displayRefill: If you took >=1 tile from the round stack: move the topmost expansion (with its leftover tiles) out to extend the display, then refill the stack's new top expansion with exactly 4 random tiles. Any expansion left with 0 tiles is flipped face up (revealing a pavilion + 1 hexagon) and becomes draftable.
acquire.storageLimit: Cannot acquire if it would exceed 12 tile or 2 expansion storage spaces.

turnFlow.startingPlayer: Youngest player starts round 1; thereafter the holder of the first-player marker starts.

variant._notes: Optional harder side: flip the scoring board and assemble the rotary wheel on its other side. Patterns score differently in Phase 2, and more bonus points are awarded for groups in final scoring. Numbers for the variant are not transcribed here.
variant._unconfirmed: Variant-side pattern scoring and group bonuses not captured numerically; out of scope for v1.
